title: Macroscepis longiflora
taxonomy: Macroscepis longiflora is classified within the family Bignoniaceae and is native to Mexico. It belongs to the order Lamiales and is a part of the eudicots. Macroscepis longiflora is further classified as a species within the genus Macroscepis. The classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its flowers and leaves. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships between different species within the Bignoniaceae family.

intro: Macroscepis longiflora, also known as Long-flowered Macroscepis, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, native to tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Macroscepis longiflora include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Macroscepis longiflora is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to treat various health conditions.
